notes.content: Between the Fall 2025 and Fall 2027 application cycles, the School of Social Sciences, Humanities and Arts will not require transfer students to complete major specific selection criteria to be admitted to this major*. Students wanting to enroll in this major will be admitted using UC Merced's general transfer requirements**. * This only applies to certain SSHA majors. ** The General Transfer Requirements are as f ol lows: Units and GPA Complete 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable college credit Earn a minimum grade point average of 2.40 (nonresident: 2.80) in UC-transferable courses Take no more than 14 semester (21 quarter) units as Pass/Not Pass Course Requirements/Subject Requirements Complete the following seven-course pattern, earning a grade of C- or better in each course: Two transferable English composition courses One transferable mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning course that has a published prerequisite of intermediate algebra Four transferable courses chosen from at least two of the following areas: Arts and Humanities, Behavioral and Social Sciences, Biological and Physical Sciences While major courses are not required for admissions, it is highly recommended to complete as many of the recommended major preparatory courses as possible before transferring to ensure timely progress to degree. Any major courses not completed prior to enrollment at UC Merced must be completed upon enrollment at UC Merced to ensure time to degree and graduation. Recommended, but not Required Prior to Transfer: POLI 001 POLI 005 POLI 010 Complete one course chosen from the following: POLI 002, 003, 006, 009 AP Exam Score & Course Exemptions An AP Government & Politics: United States: score of 4 or 5 exempts POLI 001 An AP Government & Politics: Comparative: score of 4 or 5 exempts POLI 003 UC Merced Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) credit policies are detailed in the link below: Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examinations Please read the catalog page for more information about the Political Science major. For more questions about admissions, please email: transfer@ucmerced.edu .
notes.contentHtml:
Between the Fall 2025 and Fall 2027 application cycles, the School of Social Sciences, Humanities and Arts

will not require transfer students to complete major specific selection criteria to be admitted to this major*.

Students wanting to enroll in this major will be admitted using UC Merced's general transfer requirements**.


* This only applies to certain SSHA majors.

** The General Transfer Requirements are as follows:


Units and GPA

  • Complete 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable college credit
  • Earn a minimum grade point average of 2.40 (nonresident: 2.80) in UC-transferable courses
  • Take no more than 14 semester (21 quarter) units as Pass/Not Pass


Course Requirements/Subject Requirements

Complete the following seven-course pattern, earning a grade of C- or better in each course:

  • Two transferable English composition courses
  • One transferable mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning course that has a published prerequisite of intermediate algebra
  • Four transferable courses chosen from at least two of the following areas: Arts and Humanities, Behavioral and Social Sciences, Biological and Physical Sciences


While major courses are not required for admissions, it is highly recommended to complete as many of the recommended major preparatory courses as possible before transferring to ensure timely progress to degree. Any major courses not completed prior to enrollment at UC Merced must be completed upon enrollment at UC Merced to ensure time to degree and graduation.

Recommended, but not Required Prior to Transfer:

  • POLI 001
  • POLI 005
  • POLI 010
  • Complete one course chosen from the following: POLI 002, 003, 006, 009


﻿AP Exam Score & Course Exemptions

  • An AP Government & Politics: United States: score of 4 or 5 exempts POLI 001
  • An AP Government & Politics: Comparative: score of 4 or 5 exempts POLI 003


UC Merced Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) credit policies are detailed in the link below:

Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examinations


Please read the catalog page for more information about the Political Science major.


For more questions about admissions, please email: transfer@ucmerced.edu.
